mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through lower cost of capital for corporate cash flows.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.28% as of 2008-04-01 indicates easing cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods when FEDFUNDS < 2.5% do not exceed baseline returns by at least 2% annualized (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% as of 2008-04-01 marks threshold for labor stress
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window during labor market deterioration periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) combined with falling retail sales (RSAFS) indicate weakening aggregate demand. This joint deterioration signals reduced future corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand-side recession signals that single-series measures miss.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 947k units (2008-03-01) shows housing stress
regime_hint: demand-side recession with housing and consumption collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative joint signal, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below 111.0 signals supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This indicates falling corporate profitability and future earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.1807 as of 2008-04-01 near critical threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of periods when INDPRO < 111.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window in supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market weakness that reduces household income and consumer spending power. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market participants reprrice earnings expectations downward in response to employment data releases.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and employment contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following PAYEMS declines > 0.2%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 214.0 combined with elevated federal funds rate above 2.0% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations. The interaction captures the joint effect of price pressure and policy response on forward returns.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between inflation and rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured on a quarterly basis decelerating below 2.0% annualized rate signals weakening aggregate demand and economic growth. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings growth expectations decline with GDP growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 2.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11693.1B (2008-01-01 vintage) indicates slowing growth trajectory
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of periods with GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window during low-growth regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining sharply below 950k units signals housing market collapse and reduced construction-related demand. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations for construction, materials, and consumer discretionary sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 947.0k units as of 2008-03-01 indicates housing market stress
regime_hint: housing market collapse and construction recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when HOUST < 950k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window during housing downturns.
